volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Comedy of Asses (Section 3.2.565 - Section 3.3.625b)

sections.label: Section 3.2.565
sections.original: artutos , audacis viros , valentis virgatores .
sections.translation: clever , hardened fellows [*] , sturdy stripers .

sections.label: Section 3.2.566
sections.original: Fateor profecto ut praedicas , Leonida , esse vera ;
sections.translation: Certainly I do admit , Leonida , that it is true as you say . But verily , in troth , your many misdeeds , too , may be recounted as well and truly ; where wilfully you have proved faithless to the trusting , where you have been detected in theft and scourged in public ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.570
sections.original: ubi periuraris , ubi sacro manus sis admolitus ,
sections.translation: where you have proved forsworn , where you have laid hands on sacred things , where to your masters you have full oft proved a loss , a trouble , and a disgrace , where you have stoutly denied that that was given to you which had been entrusted to you , where you have proved more faithful to your wench than to your friend , where through your hardihood you have frequently reduced to weariness eight

sections.label: Section 3.2.575
sections.original: validos lictores , ulmeis adfectos lentis virgis .
sections.translation: sturdy lictors , armed with pliant twigs of elm . ( To the AUDIENCE .) Is the compliment ill repaid in the way that I ’ ve praised my comrade ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.577
sections.original: Vt meque teque maxime atque ingenio nostro decuit .
sections.translation: Just as befits both me and yourself , and our dispositions .

sections.label: Section 3.2.578
sections.original: Iam omitte ista atque hoc quod rogo responde .
sections.translation: Now drop this , and answer me this that I ask .

sections.label: Section 3.2.578b
sections.original: Rogita quod vis .
sections.translation: Enquire of me what you please .

sections.label: Section 3.2.579
sections.original: Argenti vinginti minas habesne ?
sections.translation: Have you got the twenty silver minae ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.579b
sections.original: Hariolare .
sections.translation: Guess —

sections.label: Section 3.2.580
sections.original: edepol senem Demaenetum lepidum fuisse nobis :
sections.translation: Upon my word , the old gentleman Demaenetus , has been very obliging to us . How cleverly he pretended that I was Saurea . With the greatest difficulty did I withhold my laughter , when he rebuked the stranger , because in his absence he had been unwilling to put confidence in me ; and with what readiness did he call me Saurea the chamberlain .

sections.label: Section 3.2.585
sections.original: Mane dúm .
sections.translation: Stop a moment .

sections.label: Section 3.2.585b
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.585c
sections.original: Philaenium estne haéc quae intus exit
sections.translation: Isn ’ t this Philenium that ’ s coming from in-doors , and Argyrippus with her .

sections.label: Section 3.2.586b
sections.original: Opprime os , is est . subauscultemus .
sections.translation: Keep silence , ’ tis he ; let ’ s listen quietly to them .

sections.label: Section 3.2.587
sections.original: Lacrumantem lacinia tenet lacrumans . quidnam esse dicam ?
sections.translation: In tears , she holds him , weeping , by the lappet of his garment ; what , I wonder , am I to say is the matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.588
sections.original: taciti auscultemus .
sections.translation: Let ’ s listen in silence .

sections.label: Section 3.2.588b
sections.original: Attatae , modo hercle in mentem venit ,
sections.translation: Dear me , a thought , I ’ faith , has just come into my mind ; I very much wish I had a long stick here .

sections.label: Section 3.2.589b
sections.original: Quoi rei ?
sections.translation: For what reason ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.589c
sections.original: Qui verberarem
sections.translation: With which to beat these asses [*] , if perchance they should begin to bray out here , from within the bag .

sections.label: Section 3.3.591
sections.original: Cur me retentas ?
sections.translation: Why are you holding me back ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.591b
sections.original: Quia tui amans abeuntis egeo .
sections.translation: Because , as I love you , I cannot bear your departing .

sections.label: Section 3.3.592
sections.original: Vale .
sections.translation: Farewell .

sections.label: Section 3.3.592b
sections.original: Aliquanto ámplius valerem , si hic maneres .
sections.translation: I should fare somewhat better , if you were to remain here .

sections.label: Section 3.3.593
sections.original: Salve .
sections.translation: Blessings on you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.593b
sections.original: Salvere me iubes , quoi tu abiens offers morbum .
sections.translation: Do you wish for blessings on me , to whom you are bringing disease by your departure ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.594
sections.original: Mater supremam mihi tua dixit , domum ire iussit .
sections.translation: Your mother has bid me the last farewell [*] ; she has requested me to go home .

sections.label: Section 3.3.595
sections.original: Acerbum funus filiae faciet , si te carendum est .
sections.translation: A bitter death will she cause her daughter , if I must part from you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.596
sections.original: Homo hercle hinc exclusust foras .
sections.translation: Troth now , the man has been turned out of doors there .

sections.label: Section 3.3.596b
sections.original: Ita res est .
sections.translation: Such is the fact .

sections.label: Section 3.3.596c
sections.original: Mitte quaeso .
sections.translation: Prithee , do let me go .

sections.label: Section 3.3.597
sections.original: Quo nunc abis ? quin tu hic manes ?
sections.translation: Whither are you going now ? Why don ’ t you stay here ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.597b
sections.original: Nox , si voles , manebo .
sections.translation: This night , if you choose , I ’ ll stay .

sections.label: Section 3.3.598
sections.original: Audin hunc opera ut largus est nocturna ? nunc enim esse
sections.translation: Don ’ t you hear him ? How profuse he is of his attentions by night . But now , in the daytime , he ’ s engaged ; surely he ’ s a Solon [*]

sections.label: Section 3.3.600
sections.original: leges ut conscribat , quibus se populus teneat . gerrae !
sections.translation: to write laws whereby the public may regulate itself . Psha ! those who would be in readiness for themselves to pay obedience to his laws , would decidedly never do any good ; they would be drinking night and day .

sections.label: Section 3.3.603
sections.original: Ne iste hercle ab ista non pedem discedat , si licessit ,
sections.translation: Troth now , for sure , he wouldn ’ t budge a foot from her if she would let him , who is now in such haste , and is threatening that he ’ s going away from her .

sections.label: Section 3.3.605
sections.original: Sermoni iam finem face tuo , huius sermonem accipiam .
sections.translation: Now make an end of your talk , that I may catch his discourse .

sections.label: Section 3.3.606
sections.original: Vale .
sections.translation: Farewell !

sections.label: Section 3.3.606b
sections.original: Quo properas ?
sections.translation: Whither are you hastening ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.606c
sections.original: Bene vale , apud Orcum te videbo .
sections.translation: Kindly fare you well ! I shall see you in the other world [*] . For indeed now , so soon as I can , I shall sever myself from life .

sections.label: Section 3.3.608
sections.original: Cur tu , obsecro , immerito meo me morti dedere optas ?
sections.translation: Prithee , why , while I do not deserve it , do you wish to consign me to death ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.609
sections.original: Ego te ? quam si intellegam deficere vita , iam ipse
sections.translation: I — you ? whom , if I were to hear that you were in want of life , at once would I

sections.label: Section 3.3.610
sections.original: vitam meam tibi largiar et de mea ad tuam addam .
sections.translation: present you my own life , and from my own would add to yours .

sections.label: Section 3.3.611
sections.original: Cur ergo minitaris mihi , te vitam esse amissurum ?
sections.translation: Why , then , do you threaten that you will quit life ? For what do you suppose that I shall do , if you do that which you are talking of ? I ’ m determined to do everything exactly the same to myself that you do to yourself .

sections.label: Section 3.3.614
sections.original: Oh melle dulci dulcior tu es .
sections.translation: O ! sweeter than honey are you to me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.614b
sections.original: Certe enim tú vita es mi .
sections.translation: And surely you are my life . Embrace me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.615
sections.original: complectere .
sections.translation: I do so with pleasure .

sections.label: Section 3.3.615c
sections.original: Vtinam sic efferamur .
sections.translation: Would that thus we might be carried to the tomb .

sections.label: Section 3.3.616
sections.original: O Libane , uti miser est homo qui amat .
sections.translation: O Libanus , how wretched is the man that loves .

sections.label: Section 3.3.616b
sections.original: Immo hercle vero ,
sections.translation: Aye , but surely , faith , the man that ’ s hanging up [*] is much more wretched .

sections.label: Section 3.3.617b
sections.original: Scio qui periclum feci .
sections.translation: I know that , who have had experience of it . Let ’ s go round them : let ’ s accost them , one on the one side , one on the other .

sections.label: Section 3.3.619
sections.original: ere , salve . sed num fumus est haec mulier quam amplexare ?
sections.translation: Health to you , master . But is this female , smoke , that you are embracing ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.620
sections.original: Quidum ?
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.620b
sections.original: Quia oculi sunt tibi lacrumantes , eo rogavi .
sections.translation: Because your eyes are filled with tears ; ’ twas for that reason I asked .

sections.label: Section 3.3.621
sections.original: Patronus qui vobis fuit futurus , perdidistis .
sections.translation: One who would have been a protector to you , you have lost .

sections.label: Section 3.3.622
sections.original: Equidem hercle nullum perdidi , ideo quia numquam ullum habui .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I surely haven ’ t lost one ; for this reason , be cause I never had one .

sections.label: Section 3.3.623
sections.original: Philaenium , salve .
sections.translation: Health to you , Philenium .

sections.label: Section 3.3.623b
sections.original: Dabunt di quae velitis vobis .
sections.translation: What you desire , the Gods will give you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.624
sections.original: Noctem tuam et vini cadum velim , si optata fiant .
sections.translation: I could desire your favours , and a cask of wine , if wishes were to come to pass .

sections.label: Section 3.3.625
sections.original: Verbum cave fáxis , verbero .
sections.translation: Whip-knave , beware how you speak a word .

sections.label: Section 3.3.625b
sections.original: Tibi equidem , non mihi opto .
sections.translation: Why , ’ tis for you , not for myself , I wish it .
